content.title: Don't Rush Your Salat: Embrace Tranquility
content.subtitle: Learn a Dua to Fulfill All Your Needs in This Life and the Hereafter
content.body:
Standing straight after Ruku and sitting upright between the two Sijdahs are two essential Wajib acts of Salat. Many of us rush through these moments, neglecting these pillars, which can potentially invalidate our Salat. Yet, dedicating just 3-4 seconds is enough to avoid this mistake.

The Prophet (SAW) warned those who pray hastily and commanded that Salat be performed with tranquility. Anas (RA) narrated that when the Prophet (SAW) rose from Ruku, he would remain standing for so long that we thought he might have forgotten to prostrate (Bukhari 800).

To enhance the beauty of your Salat, recite these Duas:

After rising from Ruku (after saying "Sami'allahu liman hamidah"):

رَبَّنَا وَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ حَمْدًا كَثِيرًا طَيِّبًا مُبَارَكًا فِيهِ

(Our Lord, and to You be all praise—praise that is abundant, pure, and full of blessings.)

Between the two Sijdahs:

اللّٰهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي، وَارْحَمْنِي، وَاهْدِنِي، وَاجْبُرْنِي، وَعَافِنِي، وَارْزُقْنِي، وَارْفَعْنِي

(O Allah! Forgive me, have mercy on me, guide me, compensate my losses, grant me security, provide for me, and elevate my status.)

[Abu Dawud 1/231, No. 850; Tirmidhi 284, 285]

Let us spend a few more seconds in every upcoming Salat to make our worship truly alive. You can find all necessary Salat Duas with their meanings in the 'Dua' section of the Muslims Day app.
